name: Anaphora More vs Less
technique: anaphora
platforms: [linkedin]
tags: [anaphora, repetition, storytelling, more-vs-less, contrast]
source_month: "2025-01"
---

## Why It Works

I combine storytelling — going from the "more" down to reversing it to the "less" with anaphoras. More — More — More / Less — Less — Less / They — They — They. If you can learn to tell great stories that have two mirror image parts (More vs. Less), while making it pleasing and easy to read, you're gonna write some good content.

## Template

Everyone's obsessed with more.
More [thing 1].
More [thing 2].
More [thing 3].
More [thing 4].
But here's the problem:
True [desired outcome] isn't [common approach].
It's [counterintuitive approach].
It's having:
Less [negative 1]
Less [negative 2]
Less [negative 3]
Less [negative 4]
The most [positive trait] people I know?
They [action 1] what doesn't [value 1].
They [action 2] to what [negative outcome].
They [action 3] their [valuable resource] like [precious thing].
Because they understand a simple truth:
Your best [aspiration] isn't built by [common method].
It's built by [better method].
Until all that's left is what truly [core value].

## Example

Here's an example using the template, written from the perspective of an SAT Tutor:

Everyone's obsessed with more.
More practice tests.
More prep books.
More tutoring hours.
More stress.
But here's the problem:
True test mastery isn't quantity.
It's strategy.
It's having:
Less anxiety
Less memorization
Less cramming
Less overwhelm
The most successful students I know?
They eliminate what doesn't boost their score.
They say no to last-minute changes.
They protect their study focus like diamonds.
Because they understand a simple truth:
Your best performance isn't built by endless practice.
It's built by smart preparation.
Until all that's left is what truly works.
